elements.name: Title
texts: Ease of travel to Lough Derg

elements.name: Description
texts: A description of Lough Derg and its Pilgrimage in the American Donahoe's Magazine

elements.name: Source
texts: 'Lough Derg and Its Pilgrimages', in Donahoe's Magazine, vol. 4, p. 307

elements.name: Contributor
texts: Digitised by Google, sponsored by University of Wisconsin-Madison, archived on Hathi Trust digital library

elements.name: Text
texts: "Formerly a pilgrimage to Lough Derg must have been attended with much hardship and inconvenience, when we consider that pilgrims traveled, in many cases, barefoot, from the most remote parts of the kingdom. Considering, however, the facilities of traveling which we enjoy at the present day, the journey is now regarded as little more than a pleasant excursion trip. Good roads lead towards the lake, though, we regret to say, not to its shore ; a line of railway runs pretty close to it ; well-appointed cars in connection with each train ply between the lake and railway; in a word, pilgrims have nowadays little to complain of from fatigue or inconvenience, till they are landed on the island of Lough Derg."
